prediction.reasoning: Based on typical stock volatility patterns, assuming V (Visa) has an annualized volatility of approximately 25-30%. For a 6-day horizon, this translates to roughly 3-4% daily standard deviation. The point estimate assumes slight upward momentum typical of large-cap financial stocks. Confidence intervals are constructed using normal distribution assumptions around the point estimate, with wider intervals reflecting the inherent uncertainty in short-term stock price movements.
